title: [InstCombine] Fold values to 0 on eq-dominated paths
author: arrowten
description:
InstCombine can miss simplifications when an instruction is only used in a block that is reachable only if `Op0 == Op1`. This patch folds such values to 0.

Fixes: #143658

tests.test_body:
declare void @use(i64)

define i64 @ftp_state_list(i64 %0, i64 %1) {
  %3 = sub i64 %0, %1
  %4 = icmp eq i64 %0, %1
  br i1 %4, label %5, label %common.ret

5:                                                ; preds = %2
  tail call void @use(i64 %3)
  br label %common.ret

common.ret:                                       ; preds = %5, %2
  ret i64 %3
}

--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAddSub.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAddSub.cpp
@@ -2315,7 +2315,7 @@ Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::visitSub(BinaryOperator &I) {
       continue;
 
     if (!((Cmp->getOperand(0) == Op0 && Cmp->getOperand(1) == Op1) ||
-        (Cmp->getOperand(0) == Op1 && Cmp->getOperand(1) == Op0)))
+          (Cmp->getOperand(0) == Op1 && Cmp->getOperand(1) == Op0)))
       continue;
 
     if (Br->getSuccessor(0) != BB)

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Looks like this is common enough on llvm-opt-benchmark to be worth handling. However, I don't think this is the right place/way to do it. This should probably be handled similarly to the inverted predicates in GVN's propagateEquality. See the code starting at: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/3e93d9aeed17636e1b074e70419dc6f6fa819222/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/GVN.cpp#L2579

comments.author: arrowten

@nikic Okay I will look at the GVN code and make changes accordingly.

comments.author: cardigan1008
comments.body:
Hi, here is a related miscompilation test case:

```llvm
declare void @use(i64)

define void @src_test_context_sensitive_bug4(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp ne i64 %x, %y
  br i1 %cmp, label %ne_block, label %exit

ne_block:                                         ; preds = %entry
  %sub = sub i64 %x, %y
  call void @use(i64 %sub)
  ret void

exit:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret void
}
```

transformed by opt built on this pr:

```llvm
declare void @use(i64) local_unnamed_addr

define void @test_context_sensitive_bug4(i64 %x, i64 %y) local_unnamed_addr {
entry:
  %cmp.not = icmp eq i64 %x, %y
  br i1 %cmp.not, label %common.ret, label %ne_block

ne_block:                                         ; preds = %entry
  tail call void @use(i64 0)
  br label %common.ret

common.ret:                                       ; preds = %entry, %ne_block
  ret void
}
```

Alive2 proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/6g8Nak

> Note: This is a review assisted with a self-built agent. The reproducer was validated manually. Please let me know if anything is wrong.

This transform treats `!isKnownNonEqual(a, b)` as if it implied `a == b`, and then folds `a - b` to 0. But “cannot prove non-equal” is not a sufficient precondition for substituting 0.

- **Context sensitivity:** the `SimplifyQuery` appears to be built without a `CxtI`, so `isKnownNonEqual` can’t leverage dominating path conditions. In our case, the subtraction is in a block dominated by an `icmp ne` (i.e., only reachable when `a != b`), but that context isn’t considered.
- **Overly aggressive fold:** replacing the subtraction with 0 whenever non-equality can’t be proven is incorrect on paths where operands are known to be different.
- **Missing precondition:** the fold should only be applied on an *eq-dominated* path (guaranteed `a == b`), not merely when `a != b` can’t be proven.

Possible fixes:
- thread `CxtI` through `SimplifyQuery`/`isKnownNonEqual`,
- add a stronger dominance/path precondition for the fold,
- or reconsider the safety condition for replacing `a - b` with 0.
